title: R-22 Spearhead starfighter

The '''R-22 ''Spearhead'' starfighter''', also known as the '''Tammuz-an R-22 Spearhead fighter''' and nicknamed the "'''A-wing'''," was a state-of-the-art [[starfighter]] prototype developed by the [[Tammuz-an (species)/Legends|Tammuz-an]] [[Species/Legends|species]] as an escort fighter for their [[Tammuz-an space navy|space navy]]. Inspired by the [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighter]], its design was copied by [[Alliance Underground Engineering]] and [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] when they were developing the [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]], an evolution of the R-22 Spearhead and [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptor]].

==Characteristics==
The R-22 ''Spearhead'' [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]<ref name="Complete Vehicles" /> was an agile, state-of-the-art vessel<ref name="C-3PO">''[[C-3PO: Tales of the Golden Droid]]''</ref> inspired by the [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighter]],<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> a model of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] manufactured by [[Republic Fleet Systems]]<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> that remained the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] standard "[[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] snub" for nearly four thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> Like the ''Aurek'', the R-22's spaceframe supposedly resembled the letter A of the [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic]] alphabet, leading it to be informally nicknamed the "A-wing".<ref name="The Written Word">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref> It was favoured by [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] that enjoyed tinkering, who upgraded the R-22's flight systems and stripped it of any and all components that could be jettisoned to further increase the fighter's speed and maneuverability.<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

==History==
The R-22 Spearhead was developed by the [[Tammuz-an (species)/Legends|Tammuz-an]],<ref name="A-wing starfighter" /> a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tall [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] [[Homeworld|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Tammuz-an/Legends|same name]],<ref name="Tammuz-an">{{DB|location|tammuzan|Tammuz-an}}</ref> and was used by their [[Tammuz-an space navy|space navy]] to escort the [[Tammuz-an shipping fleet]].<ref name="R-22 spearhead" />

The R-22 Spearhead became an experimental testbed for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], nicknamed the "A-wing," shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="ITW:OT">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy]]''</ref> During one early operational test, the Rebels sent a lone R-22 to [[Mission in the Dellalt system|attack]] an unguarded [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] repair dock. The fighter performed admirably, destroying the facility before the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] [[Virulence (frigate)|''Virulence'']] could respond. The mission was later recreated as a simulation used in training new pilots.<ref name="XWingGame">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]</ref>

The [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] started deploying a number of R-22s at that time. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]] and [[PROXY]] used a two-seater R-22 to travel to [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] to help [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] and [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]] liberate the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and recruit them to the Alliance cause.<ref name="TFU2Novel" /> The fleet used them in the [[Assault on Kamino]] against the Imperial [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]].<ref name="TFU2Game">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''</ref> [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] flew Spearheads in several missions during [[Operation Skyhook]],<ref name="XWingGame" /> and at least two R-22s participated in the Battle of Yavin, one piloted by [[Jake Farrell/Legends|Jake Farrell]].<ref name="RebelAssault">''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]''</ref>

[[File:YavinHotRodGarage-ITW.jpg|thumb|left|Two R-22 Spearheads in the [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Temple]] hangar, between two disassembled [[H-60 Tempest bomber/Legends|H-60 Tempest bombers]]]]
The Spearhead was nicknamed "A-wing" among Rebel pilots. The name "A-wing" persisted when the model was modified by General [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and [[Walex Blissex/Legends|Walex Blissex]] after the Battle of Yavin into the Rebel Alliance's fast RZ-1 A-wing interceptor.

Dodonna's decision to create the RZ-1 was largely driven by the Alliance's lack of a starfighter that could match the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter's]] raw speed. Additionally, the RZ-1 was noted for its improved maneuverability over the Spearhead, so the engine design was the most likely difference between the craft.

The weapons complement might have undergone a similar change, as early Spearheads used green laser ammunition rather than the red/orange seen with most Alliance fighters.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:R22tandem.png|thumb|right|A two-seat version of the R-22 Spearhead]]

Though most sources established that the A-wing was introduced by the Rebel Alliance after the Battle of Yavin, some [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] sources deviated from this and showed A-wings in use before Yavin, notably [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']], [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'', ''[[Star Wars: Force Commander]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''. This inconsistency was eventually [[Retroactive continuity|reconciled]] in ''[[C-3PO: Tales of the Golden Droid]]'', which introduced the R-22 Spearhead as an explanation for the pre-Yavin appearances of A-wings.<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=93010&start=8519|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Leland Chee]]|date=2004-10-13|time=10:46 AM|check=1}}</ref><ref name="TFN">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23888604|board=Literature|thread=Retcons - good, bad, ugly...|user=Dan Wallace|date=2006-05-15|time=7:33 AM|quote=Hence the vessel in the Droids cartoon became an R-22 Spearhead (which was itself a nod to the R-41 Starchaser , but that's another story).|archivefile=File:DanWallaceR22-JFC.png}}</ref>
